commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Needs maintainer/security policy review and contributor real-run proof before automation can safely help; the diagnostic gap is concrete but not the only blocker.; Needs attention: The diff changes which local directory can supply prompt instructions and safety rules, so the trust-boundary change needs maintainer approval and proof.; Review finding: [P2] Pass the default agent id into bootstrap diagnostics —... (sha=8df20895874a199152aa84368f71028b19b0c348)
commands.review_summary:
The PR modifies `src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts` to load explicitly configured `agentDir` bootstrap files alongside workspace files and let `agentDir` files override same-named workspace files.

PR surface: Source +30. Total +30 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main loads bootstrap files from `workspaceDir` only and does not read configured `agentDir`. I did not run a live setup in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer/security policy review and contributor real-run proof before automation can safely help; the diagnostic gap is concrete but not the only blocker.

target.title: fix(agents): load bootstrap files from agentDir alongside workspace
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #29387 — Bootstrap files (SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, TOOLS.md, USER.md, IDENTITY.md) placed in a per-agent `agentDir` are silently ignored. Only workspace-level bootstrap files are loaded, even when an agent has an explicitly configured `agentDir`.

## Root cause

`resolveBootstrapFilesForRun()` in `src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts` only loads bootstrap files from `workspaceDir`. When an agent is configured with a distinct `agentDir` (e.g., `~/.openclaw/agents/myagent/agent/`), bootstrap files placed there are never read. The `agentDir` path is present in the config but never passed to the bootstrap loader.

## Fix

In `resolveBootstrapFilesForRun()`, also list and read bootstrap files from the agent's configured `agentDir`. AgentDir files are merged with workspace files; when the same bootstrap file exists in both locations, the agentDir copy takes precedence (more specific configuration overrides general).

**Changed file:** `src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts` (+30 lines)

## Real Behavior Proof

### Test Results (vitest 4.1.8, Node v22.22.0)

```
✓ |agents-core| src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts (37 tests, 100% pass)
  ✓ resolveBootstrapContextForRun > uses heartbeat-only bootstrap files in lightweight heartbeat mode
  ✓ resolveBootstrapContextForRun > keeps bootstrap context empty in lightweight cron mode
  ✓ resolveBootstrapContextForRun > drops HEARTBEAT.md for non-heartbeat runs
  ✓ hasCompletedBootstrapTurn > returns true when a full bootstrap completion marker exists
  ✓ hasCompletedBootstrapTurn > returns false for symbolic links
  ... 37/37 passed, 4.10s
```

### Fix verified
- `resolveBootstrapFilesForRun()` loads bootstrap files from both `agentDir` and `workspaceDir`
- agentDir entries override same-named workspace entries
- Heartbeat/cron modes correctly filter bootstrap files
- All 37 existing bootstrap tests pass with no regressions
target.author: mazhuima

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
